parsed.hypothesis: Add an automatic AX cache invalidation after every UI action to ensure grounding uses fresh AX trees.
parsed.rationale: Many failures across multiple apps are likely due to stale AX trees after UI changes (clicks, window switches, typing) causing grounding to misidentify elements. AX trees are cached for performance, but modern UIs update rapidly and the cache TTL (3s) is too long for fast agent loops. By invalidating the cache immediately after any action, the grounding will always use the latest UI state, reducing misgroundings and silent failures.

change.location: Immediately after any UI action is executed in the executeAction or executeActions function (wherever actions are dispatched to the system)
change.oldCode: // (after action execution, before next screenshot or reasoning step)
change.newCode: require('./ax-grounding').invalidateCache(); // Force fresh AX tree after every action

parsed.hypothesis: Enable the ShowUI grounding worker to improve accuracy in identifying UI elements across various apps.
parsed.rationale: The pattern of failures across multiple applications with unknown errors suggests that the current AX-grounding method is insufficient in handling dynamic or complex UI elements. By enabling the ShowUI grounding worker, the system can leverage an additional layer of visual grounding to better identify elements that AX alone cannot resolve. This should reduce the failures attributed to unidentified or misidentified UI components.
